blockers.statement: The dmc-glm-smoke reference inventory is materially incomplete. The scout scoped its grep to bin/+tests/ (plus install/.github/AGENTS/linkcheck-set) and NEVER scanned .claude/workers/providers/ or docs/. After removal, dmc-glm-smoke remains referenced in 2 live contract-class surfaces (.claude/workers/providers/PROVIDER_CONTRACT.md C5b:28 & C9:32; .claude/workers/providers/manual-import/manual-import-adapter.py PROT_RE:92) and 7 docs/*.md (DMC_EFFORT_PROVIDER_POLICY, DMC_GATE_CHECKS:50, DMC_PROVIDER_SELECTION:15, DMC_RUN_MANIFEST:26, DYNAMIC_WORKFLOW, FABLE_WORKFLOW_TRANSFER, REVIEW_PACKET_V2), none enumerated by the plan. All are inert with respect to file existence (regex-over-input / prose / git-diff-pathspec) so removal breaks nothing -- but the Risk-table mitigation 'executor re-greps dmc-glm-smoke under bin/ before closing' is scoped too narrowly to have found any of them and the Findings omit them entirely (IV.3 disclosure-ledger gap). Revise: enumerate every surviving reference, classify each inert-by-existence with rationale, broaden the executor/verifier re-grep to the whole tree (frozen surfaces excluded), and disclose the residual dangling doc/contract references.

Acceptance Criterion 1 is factually unsatisfiable for dmc-glm-smoke. AC1 (plan :161-164) asserts 'git grep for each basename returns only historical .harness/ prose + the installer/manifest heredoc line'. That holds for the five doc/zip strays (verified zero functional refs) but is FALSE for dmc-glm-smoke: after removal, git grep dmc-glm-smoke still returns ~20 frozen bin/lib/dmc-v0.* PROT_REs, the edited bin/lib/dmc-repo-intel.py, docs/MILESTONES.md, the 2 provider surfaces, and the 7 docs listed in BL-INVENTORY. A verifier taking AC1 literally must mark it UNMET; the plan sets a gate criterion its own execution cannot honestly pass. Re-word AC1's negative-proof for dmc-glm-smoke to a TRUE, satisfiable statement (e.g. git ls-files empty + git grep returns only the enumerated inert protected-path listings, none keying on file existence).
